item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that's horrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. +1

item.title: Re: This should be tagged "spooky"
item.content:encoded: And while eating pie, forgot what their own tongue was and consumed the 'fleshy organic substance of unknown origin' and subsequently bled to death…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the researchers would have been able to write what you wrote in the spoiler post

I mean, I don't think so, unless they were able to magically recall all the things that happened in the previous experiments run by random personnel, and connect those forgotten dots. Perhaps with even more testing — sure! But in this time frame? They haven't cracked the case beyond a very small, vague document, and hold no data of their own except some dead guy. Researcher competence only goes so far when what you're studying is unknowable, even with basic mnestics — and these personnel clearly ain't no Antimemetics veterans.

Also, not sure if intentional, but there is no link to 4030 or 4032. If it's a format screw, either there is something I'm really not getting or…

Yup, that's intentional! It has nothing to do with the format screw; that's just not an article requirement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Though I read all it and got it the first time, I have to -1.
It's a bit like 055, but the problem is that with the expiration of the SCP and it's effect, the researchers would have been able to write what you wrote in the spoiler post, meaning basically I'd expect something like "this is an expired version of SCP-4031 documentation, enter credentials here for the current version" or some such thing.

Also, not sure if intentional, but there is no link to 4030 or 4032. If it's a format screw, either there is something I'm really not getting or… yeah, moving on.

item.title: This should be tagged "spooky"
item.content:encoded:
Potential Spoilers
 

When I finally understood it, the whole amnesiac-amnestic thing really freaked me out. The addendum really did it in for me. So, essentially, they forgot themselves?

item.title: Re: Thank you for the explanation
item.content:encoded: Still shows a downvote on my screen!

item.title: Thank you for the explanation
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. Thanks for explaining it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice to see the offic explanation, I'm happier now than ever with my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I've had more than enough people tell me they don't get this article, so I suppose it's time to let the cat out of the bag and explain it.

spoiler
– hide block

SCP-4031 is titled The Amnesiac Redemption. What is an "amnesiac"? An amnesiac is a person with amnesia. In the early days of the SCP Wiki, the fictional drugs that make people forget things were known as amnesiacs. It didn't take long for the standard consensus of this to turn on its head, allowing the Wiki to adopt the word "amnestic" (a more correct term) in place of "amnesiac".

This article is based on that semantic confusion.

What is SCP-4031? SCP-4031 is an amnesiac: a person with amnesia. This person has the properties of an amnestic: any subject in sensory or memetic range becomes confused, forgetful, and gets a bad taste in their mouth. This amnesiac also acts like an amnestic: their amnestic properties are fast-acting. And because you become forgetful when you're in range, researchers were woefully unable to determine any physical or quantitative properties beyond the fact that it acts like an amnestic.

The article is intentionally masked with confusion. From the perspective of the reader (a fellow researcher, presumably), it looks like the research team was unable to mount any proper efforts toward investigating the anomaly. The threat level is undetermined, there are no containment procedures, and the description is a few sentences long. The average reader won't immediately get that this is an amnesiac and not a proper amnestic — the words are close enough in spelling. And if it quacks like an amnestic, and walks like an amnestic…

The only thing that might make the unsuspecting reader think something is off is the fact that the amnesiac makes male vocalizations. Amnestics don't typically do that, do they?

Glossing over the fun of the experimentation log (it's based on the conceit that subjects — not necessarily people, but test subjects — become forgotten when they make contact with the amnestic amnesiac), we get to the addenda. Researchers consume mnestics: drugs that make you remember.

And the veil over this anomaly is lifted: it was a guy with memory loss causing all this confusion. How did he get memory loss? "Subject possessed significant cranial damage". But what else do they find? "Subject possessed lacerations, emaciation, a broken left clavicle, and blood loss in the oral cavity." Over the course of experimenting with this guy (note that there's no passage of time here — testing could have occurred across three hours, three days, or three weeks), they gravely injured him. And because he's an amnesia patient that makes other people forget about him, he couldn't stop the testing, and the researchers could never figure out what horrible things they were doing.

item.title: I don’t understand this...
item.content:encoded: Downvoted for being nonsensical and confusing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice, upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone who really doesn’t get it, I can’t really say too much. It’s kinda obvious that its’s a riff on 2719, but I feel that hit these same beats a lot better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
well not really but it doesnt have much to differenciate itself from 2719
and hiding the catch with "of unknown makeup, quantity, and origin." feels forced if you ask me (in my worthless opinion)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: its still a bootleg 2719 though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I can appreciate an 2719 homage on its own merits, but this doesn't have the same twisted internal logic that can sustain a narrative flow in the testing logs, nor is the twist really all that exciting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is awesome.

I don't understand it, but that's just 'cause I'm professionally dumb. I'll get back to you with a better comment once I figure it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I thought this was going to be a bootleg version of the inside scp, but the addendum definitely saved it imo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, this is the first article in a long while to

1: make me think

2: make me feel rewarded when i discovered what the twist was

So im upvoting on that front. Very nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for personal reasons; this is risky and panicky and it is very tangible.

item.title: SCP-4031: The Amnesiac Redemption
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4031: The Amnesiac Redemption [Audio version]
Author: Lt Flops

Written for Bright's 1st Challenge. It went from concept to posting in under 3 hours.

"Amnesiac" is not a typo. Please don't try to fix it — the page is locked to prevent you from doing so.

▷ Special Thanks

Special Thanks

▷ pxdnbluesoul, for providing feedback on SCP Café's recap episode 2018, Week 52 (Part 2).
▷ Woedenaz and stormbreath, for CSS assistance.

△ Close

▷ Declassification, by Brewsterion 1017

Accompanying Article(s)

▷ A Kind of Redemption, by Tufto

If you liked this, check out my Author Page!
